202	- Follow-up `us_011_fix_silent_no_secret` rewrites the daily cron's body as a `do $body$ ... $body$` PL/pgSQL block that resolves both vault secrets first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` if either is NULL — fixes a Medium reviewer finding (pg_net silently returns success on `url := NULL`, so a missing vault secret would have logged as a successful run forever instead of surfacing in `cron.job_run_details`). Promoted both the pg_net NULL-trap and the vault-secret pattern to Codebase Patterns.
203	- Verified via `execute_sql` DO block: synthetic 25h-old `fresh`+`is_latest` snapshot flips to `stale` after running the marker body verbatim. Confirmed both jobs registered with `active=true` in `cron.job` (jobid 1 = mark-metrics-stale, jobid 2 = cron-refresh-metrics).
204	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417163000_us_011_cron_jobs.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` regenerated — byte-identical (cron jobs aren't surfaced in the public schema).
205	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
206	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Medium: silent vault-NULL no-op resolved by the follow-up migration). Reviewer also surfaced two spec-gap notes (orphaned non-`is_latest` fresh rows; §16 has 4 unscheduled jobs) — both promoted to Codebase Patterns rather than fixed in this story per one-story-per-iteration.
207	- Spec gap (story vs spec naming): story AC said "metrics-refresh edge function"; spec §5.2 distinguishes the user-facing `metrics-refresh` (US-021) from the internal `cron-refresh-metrics`. Followed spec naming; the cron deliberately points at the internal function (different rate-limit + auth shape).
208	- Spec gap (§16 unscheduled jobs): see Codebase Patterns. `fail-stuck-refreshing` is the most operationally important — should land with the webhook receiver story group (US-016+).
209	- Spec gap (staleness marker orphan rows): see Codebase Patterns. UI unaffected.
210	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
211	  - pg_net's silent-success-on-NULL is the kind of footgun where the obvious code path looks fine but the failure mode is invisible. When a cron body depends on an external lookup (vault, dynamic config, etc.), wrap it in PL/pgSQL with explicit NULL handling — never inline the lookup as a sub-select directly in `net.http_post(url := ...)`.
212	  - `pg_cron` has no upsert; the `do $$ ... if exists ... then perform cron.unschedule(...) ...` pattern is the standard idempotency wrapper. Save it for any future cron migration.
213	  - `create extension pg_net with schema extensions` is accepted but pg_net always installs functions in the `net` schema regardless of WITH SCHEMA. Document this in the migration header so the next reader doesn't go hunting in `extensions.net.http_post`.
214	  - When the reviewer flags a spec-level gap (not a code bug), promote it to Codebase Patterns rather than fixing it in the current story. The one-story-per-iteration rule trumps "while we're here".
215	---
216	
217	## 2026-04-17 17:05 — US-012
218	- Applied `us_012_seed` via Supabase MCP: one DO block seeds fixed-UUID rows — `acme_studio` lister + `lister_profiles`, `seed_creator` creator + `creator_profiles` + tiktok/instagram social_links, one active listing "Promote Acme Headphones" with listing_version v1 (2 pre-conditions — `min_followers` tiktok/instagram, 2 post-conditions — `post_family_friendly` bool + `post_must_mention` contains, 1 sample_video), and three metric_snapshots (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts) that the US-007 BEFORE-INSERT trigger mirrors into creator_profiles.
219	- Verified via `execute_sql`: creator_profiles denorm columns all populated — tiktok_follower_count=42000, tiktok_avg_views_last_10=18500, tiktok_total_likes=850000, tiktok_video_count=120, tiktok_is_verified=false, instagram_follower_count=15000, instagram_avg_views_last_10=6400, instagram_media_count=55, metrics_fetched_at set. All three metric_snapshots rows `is_latest=true` (one per scrape_mode). listing.current_version_id wired to v1 without re-triggering the US-010 cascade (the follow-up UPDATE touches only current_version_id — no versioned-field diff).
220	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417170000_us_012_seed.sql` (new). `src/types/supabase.ts` unchanged (seed is pure DML — no DDL).
221	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
222	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One cosmetic note (`is_verified=true` on the ig_details row is silently discarded by the denorm trigger since `instagram_is_verified` is not a modeled denorm column — stored in snapshot but harmless; not worth a follow-up migration given append-only rules).
223	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
224	  - Fixed UUIDs for seeded rows (`11111111-1111-1111-1111-1111111110XX` pattern) let later stories reference the seed without having to query-by-handle first. Later stories that rely on the seed (US-038 discover feed filter, US-055 edit-campaign cascade verification, US-057 override-eligibility approval) should import these IDs as constants rather than re-querying.
225	  - Safe pattern for seeding an `active` listing without triggering the US-010 cascade: (a) INSERT listing with `status='active'` + `current_version_id=NULL`, (b) INSERT listing_versions v1, (c) UPDATE only `current_version_id` on the listing. The UPDATE fires `trg_bump_listing_version` but `price_cents`/`currency`/`max_submissions` are unchanged and `version_bump_reason` is NULL, so `versioned_changed=false` and no cascade runs. Avoids the alternative of disabling/re-enabling the trigger mid-migration.
226	  - `denorm_metrics` only mirrors columns that exist on `creator_profiles`. Extra columns on the snapshot (e.g. `is_verified` on `ig_details`) are legal but silently ignored — no Instagram verification denorm column exists per spec §4.7. Don't fabricate values for snapshot columns that don't map anywhere.
227	---
228	
229	## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
230	- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
231	- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
232	- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
233	- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
234	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
235	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
236	- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
237	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
238	  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
239	  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
240	  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
241	  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.
242	---
243	
244	## 2026-04-17 13:00 — US-008
245	- Applied `us_008_notifications_events` via Supabase MCP: `public.notifications` (6 cols, inbox + unread partial indexes), `public.push_tokens` (6 cols, unique `expo_token`, ios/android check), `public.events` (bigserial PK audit log with composite `(entity, entity_id, created_at desc)` index). RLS enabled on all three; policies deferred to US-009. `revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated` enforces app-role append-only per spec §4.4 line 1086.
246	- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts`: three new tables appear with correct shapes (events.id → `number` from bigserial; notifications.kind → `notification_kind` enum; push_tokens.platform → `string` since the spec uses a CHECK not an enum).
247	- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417130000_us_008_notifications_events.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
248	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables + the 12 pre-existing — all deferred to US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
249	- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. One medium observation flagged: append-only enforcement scope for `events` — migration matches the spec literally but service_role retains UPDATE/DELETE grants. Promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-016+ handoff (edge functions writing to events must discipline themselves to INSERT-only; no DB trigger backstop exists).
250	- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.

file.content:
# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.
- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).
- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).
- **Edge-function smoke tests live under `scripts/test/<fn-name>.ts`, invoked with `bun run`.** Read `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from `process.env` (bun auto-loads `.env`). Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.
- **Apify REST endpoint + slug encoding:** use `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<rest-slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_KEY>&waitForFinish=<secs>`. REST slugs replace `/` with `~` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`). `waitForFinish` makes Apify block up to N seconds before returning the run object — if the run exceeds N, Apify returns `status=RUNNING` and webhooks pick up the terminal state later. Response shape is `{ data: { id, defaultDatasetId, status, ... } }`. For this project the wrapper at `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` uses direct REST (not the `apify-client` SDK) because Deno in Edge Runtime handles plain fetch cleanly and `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` is the sanctioned way to read the token.
- **Never echo external API error bodies into thrown Error strings when the request URL carries a secret in the query string.** Apify (and several other providers) sometimes include the request URL in error payloads, so `throw new Error(\`... ${body}\`)` can land the token in edge-function logs. Throw a fixed-string error with status + slug only; if you need the body for debugging, log it separately with redaction.
- **Deno tests for `_shared/` modules live alongside them as `<name>.test.ts` and run with `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/<name>.test.ts`.** Assertions come from `jsr:@std/assert@1`. Mock fetch by replacing `globalThis.fetch` with a spy that records `{url, init}` and returns a canned `Response`; always restore in a `try/finally`. Deno isn't on every dev machine; `brew install deno` on macOS is a one-liner. If Deno isn't available in a given iteration, document it in the progress entry and have the CI for this run the tests.
- **Public async wrappers must be declared `async` if they do any synchronous validation that might throw**, so the throw surfaces as a Promise rejection rather than a synchronous exception. `assertRejects` in `@std/assert` fails with "Function throws when expected to reject" if the function throws synchronously — a real footgun that can only be caught by actually running the test.
- **Constant-time dual-secret compare for rotation-aware webhook auth:** edge functions that authenticate third-party webhooks via a shared secret must (a) compare in constant time (XOR-accumulate over `max(len(a), len(b))` bytes, iterate the longer one), (b) check BOTH `<SECRET>` and `<SECRET>_PREVIOUS` env vars without short-circuit, and (c) return false if neither env var is set. The second point is subtle: `return timingSafeEqual(a, current) || timingSafeEqual(a, previous)` short-circuits when the first matches, which means an attacker who has recovered `current` can time-distinguish whether `previous` is configured. Correct shape is `let matchA = current ? timingSafeEqual(provided, current) : false; let matchB = previous ? timingSafeEqual(provided, previous) : false; return matchA || matchB` — both evaluated unconditionally. Reference: `supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts` `verifyWebhookSecret`.
- **Third-party-webhook-receiver edge functions deploy with `verify_jwt=false`** (Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function({verify_jwt: false})`). The provider cannot send an `Authorization: Bearer <marketify-jwt>` — authentication is via the provider's own header (e.g. `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret`). The Supabase API gateway still requires a valid project `apikey` though, so smoke tests must send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers in addition to the webhook-specific auth header. For Apify specifically, pair this with a `payloadTemplate` in the per-run webhook registration that injects `social_link_id` / `scrape_mode` / `run_id` / `apify_finished_at` — these fields are NOT in Apify's default event shape and are required by our receiver contract.
- **Webhook-receiver idempotency via service-role RPC + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING:** the receiver's persistence step goes through a PL/pgSQL RPC that `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (<run-id-col>) WHERE <run-id-col> IS NOT NULL DO NOTHING` and returns jsonb `{inserted: bool, duplicate: bool, <id>?: uuid}`. Paired with a partial unique index on the run-id column, replays of the same webhook are safe. Revoke the RPC from anon/authenticated/public and grant only to service_role. The RPC is invoked by the edge function with a service-role Supabase client — NO `SECURITY DEFINER` needed because service_role already has BYPASSRLS. Reference: `public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile` + `metric_snapshots_run_uniq` (US-006/016).
- **Scope env-var reads to the branch that actually needs them.** A single top-of-handler `Deno.env.get("OPTIONAL_KEY")` guard that 500s on missing keys will trip branches that don't need the key. For the apify-webhook, `APIFY_KEY` is only needed to fetch the dataset on the SUCCEEDED branch — gating the whole handler on it broke the FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED branches (which have no dataset). Move the lookup inside the conditional, and only return `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` when you actually can't proceed.
- **`supabase/functions/<name>-live.ts` convention for Apify-touching e2e tests:** any smoke test that spends real Apify credits lives alongside the cheap-path smoke as `<fn>-live.ts`, is NOT part of the default test suite, and documents what it costs + what external state it touches. US-016 used `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` against `@tiktok` (cheap + stable + public) with seed `social_link_id = 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020` — good reference fixture for future Apify-touching stories. DB-side verification (snapshot row + denorm trigger) is done separately via MCP `execute_sql` after the script completes.
- **Apify `payloadTemplate` requires `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` for quoted `"{{placeholder}}"` substitution.** Without this flag, quoted placeholders stay literal (`"{{resource.id}}"` delivered as-is) and the receiver's validation layer 400s. Bare unquoted `{{var}}` substitutes raw JSON but breaks `JSON.stringify(payloadTemplate)` because the placeholder isn't valid JSON. Rule: every webhook spec we build has `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` and uses quoted placeholders exclusively. Reference: `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` `ApifyWebhookSpec` + `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`.
- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Don't special-case only RUNNING.

## 2026-04-17 17:45 — US-013
- Added `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (HS256 sign/verify via Web Crypto) and `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (shared `corsHeaders` const). JWT claim shape is `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 though the story AC listed only 4 claims; spec wins.
- `verifyJwt` rejects non-HS256 `alg`, missing claims, `exp ≤ now`, and `iat > now + 60s`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` with a `["verify"]`-only key (not re-sign + constant-time compare) per reviewer feedback. Base64url decode wraps `atob` in try/catch so malformed inputs surface as `"Malformed JWT"` rather than DOMExceptions.
- Excluded `supabase/**` from the Expo toolchain: `tsconfig.json` now has `"exclude": ["node_modules", "supabase/**/*"]`; `eslint.config.js` ignores the same glob. Edge functions run in Deno and import `Deno.env`, so they belong outside the RN typecheck. Added `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET=` to `.env.example` (secret to be populated out-of-band).
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (new), `supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts` (new), `tsconfig.json`, `eslint.config.js`, `.env.example`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp n/a (no UI)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Important: (1) `verifyJwt` rewritten to use `crypto.subtle.verify` with verify-only key, eliminating the dual-use key smell; (2) base64 decode + JSON parses wrapped so malformed JWTs raise `"Malformed JWT"` consistently).
- Spec gap: story AC lists `{sub, role, iat, exp}` as claims; spec §4.4 lists `{sub, role, session_id}`. Included all five — `session_id` is load-bearing for §15c revocation. Flagged in commit reasoning so future iterations don't "simplify" it away.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Web Crypto's `crypto.subtle.verify` is the preferred verify path — it's constant-time by construction and lets you import the key with minimal `["verify"]` usage. Only reach for manual `sign`-then-constant-time-compare when the API you need (e.g. key fingerprints) isn't available.
  - `atob` throws DOMException on malformed base64, not a JS Error. Any edge-function helper that decodes untrusted input must wrap `atob` in try/catch and rethrow a normalized Error so callers can uniformly `.catch(e => e.message)`.
  - The tsc `exclude` pattern does NOT stop files from being pulled in via transitive imports; it only filters the project-file glob. For runtime-split code (RN vs Deno), the exclusion holds only as long as app code never imports from the excluded tree. Add a comment on the boundary if this becomes a recurring temptation.
  - When an acceptance criterion and the authoritative spec disagree on claim shape (or any other contract detail), the spec wins per Ralph rules — but note the deviation in both the commit reasoning and Codebase Patterns so the next iteration doesn't try to reconcile back toward the story text.

file.content:
- Spec gap: Story AC names only "notifications and events tables"; spec §4.7 block 12 bundles `push_tokens` alongside `notifications`. Followed spec (same precedent as US-005's `listing_conditions` vs story's split pre/post). Push notifications are scoped to v1.1 per product-plan §3.1 line 120 — the table is idle but the schema shape is locked in now.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Tables that the spec defines for future-version features (e.g. push_tokens for v1.1) should still be created when they appear in the same §4.7 block as an in-scope table. Partial block creation causes drift that later iterations would have to reconcile.

- Smoke test at `scripts/test/auth-signup-creator.ts` covers all 5 AC branches: 200 happy path, 409 USERNAME_TAKEN, 422 HANDLE_REQUIRED, 400 INVALID_REQUEST, 409 HANDLE_TAKEN (via `@`-prefixed handle — also proves normalization). Self-cleans via service-role DELETE when `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` is available; else logs and moves on. End-to-end verified via PL/pgSQL DO block first (all 4 branches plus only-IG) then via the live deployed function.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417220000_us_019_auth_signup_creator_fn.sql` (new), `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts` (new), `scripts/test/auth-signup-creator.ts` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only the expected INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events` — intentional per US-008) / mobile-mcp n/a (edge-function-only story).
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved. Three observations surfaced, all below the medium+ bar for blocking: (1) response shape uses `{token, user_id, role}` per story AC + sibling `auth-signup-lister` precedent, diverging from `docs/tech-architecture.md` §5.1 which names the token field `jwt` and adds `social_links[]` + `initial_scrape_queued` — spec gap; story AC wins, but the client will need to match. (2) Error-code name `HANDLE_REQUIRED` vs spec's `MISSING_HANDLE` — another naming drift the client must match. (3) Smoke test did not self-clean originally — fixed in this commit by adding a service-role DELETE teardown gated on `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`.
- Spec gap: response body contract drifted between `docs/tech-architecture.md` §5.1 (Res shape: `{user_id, jwt, social_links[], initial_scrape_queued}`) and the explicit story AC (`{token, user_id, role}`). US-014's lister already shipped with the story-AC shape; keeping the pair consistent. When US-033/US-034 (signup screens) wire the clients, the client must expect `token` not `jwt` and must fetch social_links separately until US-020 wraps this RPC. Similarly, error code names `USERNAME_TAKEN` / `HANDLE_TAKEN` / `HANDLE_REQUIRED` / `INVALID_REQUEST` / `INVALID_JSON` should be treated as the source of truth for the client — spec's `MISSING_HANDLE` name is stale.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The "atomic multi-insert via single RPC" pattern scales cleanly from 2-table (lister: users + lister_profiles) to 3-table (creator: users + creator_profiles + 0-2 social_links) — the exception-handler constraint mapping is the only per-story work. Reuse this shape for any future multi-table-insert edge function.
  - Handle normalization (strip leading `@`, trim, nullify empty) belongs in the edge function, NOT the RPC — keeps the RPC parameter shape matching the raw DB column type (`citext`) and keeps the cleaning logic observable at the HTTP boundary.
  - The partial unique indexes on `social_links` (`social_links_user_platform_uniq`, `social_links_platform_handle_uniq`) both fire as `unique_violation` with different `constraint_name` — handle both in the exception mapping. A user who retries with the same handle-platform combo trips `_user_platform_uniq`; someone else trying to claim the same handle trips `_platform_handle_uniq`. Both surface as HANDLE_TAKEN from the creator's perspective (they don't know they collided with themselves).
  - Smoke tests for creator-signup-style flows need a service-role teardown; the anon key can't bypass the RLS on `public.users`. Gate the teardown on `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` being set so the test still runs (and surfaces errors) in environments without the key, just without cleanup.
---

## 2026-04-17 22:20 — US-020
- Extended `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts` to dispatch up to 3 parallel Apify runs (tiktok_profile via `runTikTokProfile`, ig_details via `runInstagramDetails`, ig_posts via `runInstagramPosts`) via `Promise.allSettled` after the signup RPC commits. Each run registers an ad-hoc webhook pointing at `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/apify-webhook` with `payloadTemplate` injecting `{social_link_id, scrape_mode, run_id:"{{resource.id}}", apify_finished_at:"{{resource.finishedAt}}"}` and `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header. Response now includes `metrics_status: { tiktok, ig_details, ig_posts }` — each key is `'fresh'` (run SUCCEEDED within waitForFinish=60), `'refreshing'` (READY/RUNNING/TIMING-OUT/ABORTING — webhook will fire later), `'failed'` (terminal non-success), or `'skipped'` (no handle supplied). Partial failure is non-fatal — any run that rejects marks that platform 'failed' and the signup response still 200s.
- Added `ApifyWebhookSpec` interface + `shouldInterpolateStrings?: boolean` field to `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts`, plus webhook-option threading into `startActorRun` (base64-encodes JSON array into `webhooks` query param per Apify REST contract). Three new Deno tests cover the webhook-encoding branch (present / undefined / empty-array omission).
- Live e2e verified end-to-end on the deployed v5 function: POST → 200 in ~15s with `metrics_status: { tiktok:"fresh", ig_details:"fresh", ig_posts:"fresh" }`; `metric_snapshots` confirmed via `execute_sql` — 3 rows (status='fresh') appeared for the new user_id within ~1 minute. Pipeline: auth-signup-creator → `auth_signup_creator` RPC → Apify actor run (waitForFinish=60s) → ad-hoc webhook → `apify-webhook` receiver → scrape-mode-specific persist RPC → denorm trigger.
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`, `scripts/test/auth-signup-creator.ts`, `scripts/test/auth-signup-creator-live.ts` (new).
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/apify.test.ts` ✓ (11 pass) / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings) / mobile-mcp n/a (edge-function-only story).
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (Issue 1 — status classifier originally mapped only RUNNING/READY to 'refreshing' and bucketed TIMING-OUT + ABORTING into 'failed', but all four are Apify transitional statuses whose webhooks will still fire. Extended the 'refreshing' branch to cover all four per Apify docs; comment updated to list each and explain why they map to 'refreshing'. Redeployed as v5 and re-ran the live e2e — all three platforms still 'fresh' within 60s).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Both new learnings (Apify `shouldInterpolateStrings` flag + full transitional-status list) promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - `Promise.allSettled` is the right primitive for partial-failure-tolerant multi-dispatch: a single creator with {tiktok, ig} handles gets up to 3 Apify runs in flight, each independent, and the response reflects per-run outcome without any single failure 500-ing the whole signup.
  - Ad-hoc webhook registration via Apify REST is preferred over dashboard-configured "standby" webhooks for this flow because it lets the `payloadTemplate` inject `social_link_id` + `scrape_mode` as query-scoped constants — the receiver doesn't need a separate mapping layer from `run_id → (social_link_id, scrape_mode)`.

60-### 1.6 Metrics / Apify
61-
62-| Area | Decision |
63-|---|---|
64:| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
65-| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
66-| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
67-| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
68-
69-### 1.7 Security / Abuse
--
342-
343-### 3f. Cost & Rate Management
344-
345-- Every actor run costs Apify credits. Published pricing at time of writing: TikTok Scraper ≈ $0.40 / 1k videos (so ~$0.004 per 10-video run), Instagram Scraper ≈ $2.30 / 1k results. Budget conservatively at ~$0.01 per TikTok refresh and ~$0.02 per IG refresh (details + 10 posts = 11 results).
346-- **Scale math (back-of-envelope):** 10k creators × daily cron refresh ≈ $40/day TikTok-only, $200/day IG-only, $240/day fully-linked. That's ~$7.2k/month worst case. Sensitivity: cutting daily cron to every 48h halves that; capping free-tier creators to weekly refresh quarters it.
347:- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` returned before any Apify call.
348-[Omitted long context line]
349-- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
350-- Memory: default `memoryMbytes` is fine for both actors (1024 MB); don't override.
351-
352-### 3g. Webhook Security

content.content:
1620	```
1621	Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` (creator must keep ≥1 linked platform).
1622	
1623	**`POST /metrics/refresh`** — creator-triggered, rate-limited
1624	```ts
1625	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1626	interface Res {
1627	  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
1628	  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
1629	  retry_after_sec?: number;
1630	}
1631	```
1632	Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1633	
1634	| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
1635	|---|---|---|
1636	| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
1637	| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |
1638	
1639	Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
1640	
1641	**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
1642	- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
1643	- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
1644	- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
1645	- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
1646	  ```sql
1647	  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
1648	  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
1649	  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
1650	  -- Apify runs are queued.
1651	  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
1652	  ```
1653	  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.
1654	
1655	### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver
1656	
1657	**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
1658	```ts
1659	interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
1660	  eventType:
1661	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'
1662	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'
1663	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT'
1664	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED';
1665	  resource: { id: string; defaultDatasetId: string; status: string; actId: string; };
1666	  // injected via payloadTemplate when we started the run:
1667	  social_link_id: string;
1668	  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
1669	  run_id: string;
1670	  status: string;
1671	  dataset_id: string;
1672	}
1673	interface Res { ok: true; }
1674	```
1675	Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).
1676	
1677	Flow:
1678	1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
1679	2. If `eventType !== 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'`: prepare `status_next=failed`, fill `error_message`, short-circuit to step 5.
1680	3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
1681	4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
1682	5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
1683	   ```sql
1684	   update public.metric_snapshots
1685	      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
1686	          raw_payload   = :items,
1687	          error_message = :error_message,
1688	          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
1689	          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
1690	          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
1691	    where apify_run_id = :run_id
1692	      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
1693	   ```
1694	   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
1695	6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
1696	7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.
1697	
1698	**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. file.content:
```
Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` (creator must keep ≥1 linked platform).

**`POST /metrics/refresh`** — creator-triggered, rate-limited
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res {
  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
  retry_after_sec?: number;
}
```
Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.

| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
|---|---|---|
| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |

Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.

**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
  ```sql
  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
  -- Apify runs are queued.
  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
  ```
  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.

### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver

**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
```ts
interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
  eventType:
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT'
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED';
  resource: { id: string; defaultDatasetId: string; status: string; actId: string; };
  // injected via payloadTemplate when we started the run:
  social_link_id: string;
  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
  run_id: string;
  status: string;
  dataset_id: string;
}
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).

Flow:
1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
2. If `eventType !== 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'`: prepare `status_next=failed`, fill `error_message`, short-circuit to step 5.
3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
   ```sql
   update public.metric_snapshots
      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
          raw_payload   = :items,
          error_message = :error_message,
          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
    where apify_run_id = :run_id
      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
   ```
   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.

**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked the Ralph autonomous loop for the Marketify project (Expo + React Native + Supabase marketplace MVP). Ralph's rules require picking exactly ONE story per iteration where `passes: false` (lowest priority number), implementing the minimal change set to satisfy all acceptance criteria, running quality gates (tsc, lint, supabase advisors), spawning the feature-dev:code-reviewer subagent, mobile-mcp verification for UI stories, committing with `feat: [US-XXX] Title` format, updating `scripts/ralph/prd.json` to mark `passes: true`, and appending a dated entry to `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. The current story is **US-021: Edge function metrics-refresh with 6h throttle** — the next `passes: false` story. All DB-schema stories (US-003 through US-012), JWT helper (US-013), and edge functions auth-signup-lister (US-014), apify shared wrapper (US-015), apify-webhook receiver (US-016/017/018), auth-signup-creator (US-019/020) are already shipped.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Expo SDK 54 + React Native + expo-router + Reanimated 4 + NativeWind (not used)
   - Supabase (project ref `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region `ap-southeast-1`): Postgres 17, RLS, pg_cron, pg_net, Edge Functions (Deno)
   - Custom HS256 JWTs via `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (distinct from Supabase's JWT secret) with claims `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}`
   - Apify REST API: slugs use `~` instead of `/` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`); ad-hoc per-run webhooks via `?webhooks=<base64(JSON.stringify(specs))>` query param; `payloadTemplate` with `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` for quoted placeholders
   - Denormalization trigger `app_private.denorm_metrics` fires on metric_snapshots INSERT, mirroring columns per scrape_mode into creator_profiles with pg_advisory_xact_lock
   - 6h throttle keyed on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at` (spec §5.2)
   - Service-role supabase client in edge functions; RLS bypassed for admin operations
   - Constant-time dual-secret compare for rotation-aware webhook auth
   - Webhook receiver idempotency via partial unique index on apify_run_id + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
   - Edge-function file deploy: entrypoint at `source/index.ts`, shared utils at `_shared/*.ts` (sibling dir so `../_shared/` imports resolve)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`scripts/ralph/prd.json`** — Read to identify US-021 as next `passes: false`. AC: "POST with JWT; reads (user_id, platform) from body+claims; 6h throttle via metric_snapshots.created_at; 429 with retry_after_seconds; otherwise kick Apify; test back-to-back → second=429."
   
   - **`scripts/ralph/progress.txt`** — Read (both top Codebase Patterns section + entries through US-020). Critical patterns referenced: Apify REST + slug encoding, webhook receiver spec gap for pre-`refreshing` rows, secret management via CLI, edge-function smoke test convention, service-role RPC pattern.
   
   - **`docs/tech-architecture.md` §5.2** — Spec says:
     ```ts
     interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
     interface Res {
       snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram
       status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
       retry_after_sec?: number;
     }
     ```
     Throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`. Errors `429 RATE_LIMIT`, `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
   
   - **`supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`** (US-019/020) — Read to mirror its Apify dispatch pattern. Uses Promise.allSettled over up to 3 tasks (tiktok_profile + ig_details + ig_posts), APIFY_WAIT_SECS=60, APIFY_TERMINAL_EVENTS array, webhook spec with `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` and quoted placeholders. Status classification: `SUCCEEDED` → 'fresh'; `READY|RUNNING|TIMING-OUT|ABORTING` → 'refreshing'; else 'failed'.
   
   - **`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`** — Read. Exports `verifyJwt(token)` that enforces HS256, valid signature, required claims, exp>now, iat≤now+60s.
   
   - **`supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts`** — Read. Exports `runTikTokProfile(handle, {waitSecs?, webhooks?})`, `runInstagramDetails(...)`, `runInstagramPosts(...)` each returning `{runId, datasetId, status}`. `waitSecs` validated `Number.isFinite(waitSecs) && waitSecs >= 0`.
   
   - **`supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts`** — CREATED (310 lines). Full implementation:
     - Header contract comment documenting all response codes (200, 400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 429, 500, 502) and spec gaps
     - `THROTTLE_SECS = 6 * 60 * 60`, `APIFY_WAIT_SECS = 60`, UUID regex validation
     - Auth flow: extract Bearer token → `verifyJwt` → assert `claims.role === "creator"`
     - Body parse: `social_link_id` must be string matching UUID_RE
     - Env check: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `APIFY_KEY`, `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — all four required before any mutation
     - DB lookup: `supabase.from("social_links").select("id,user_id,platform,handle,status,last_scrape_attempt_at").eq("id", socialLinkId).maybeSingle()`
     - 404 if row missing OR row.user_id !== claims.sub (not 403 — avoids UUID existence leak)
     - 422 if `row.status === "unlinked"`
     - 6h throttle check: if `(nowMs - Date.parse(last_scrape_attempt_at))/1000 < THROTTLE_SECS` → 429 with `retry_after_sec: THROTTLE_SECS - elapsedSec`
     - Bump `last_scrape_attempt_at = new Date(nowMs).toISOString()` via service-role UPDATE BEFORE dispatch (protects Apify budget from spam via flaky path)
     - Dispatch via `dispatchApifyRuns` — mirrors US-020: TikTok=1 task (tiktok_profile), Instagram=2 tasks (ig_details + ig_posts), Promise.allSettled, per-run webhook with payloadTemplate injecting `social_link_id`, `scrape_mode`, `run_id`, `dataset_id`
     - Returns `{status: "queued", snapshot_ids: [] as string[], metrics_status}` — snapshot_ids deliberately empty per documented spec gap

   - **`/tmp/metrics-refresh-deploy.json`** — Built via Node helper script:
     ```js
     const files = [
       { name: 'source/index.ts', content: fs.readFileSync('supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts','utf8') },
       { name: '_shared/jwt.ts', content: fs.readFileSync('supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts','utf8') },
       { name: '_shared/cors.ts', content: fs.readFileSync('supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts','utf8') },
       { name: '_shared/apify.ts', content: fs.readFileSync('supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts','utf8') },
     ];
     ```
     22,773 bytes containing all 4 files with proper relative names to satisfy Deno module resolution.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Initial deploy error**: `deploy_edge_function` with only `index.ts` failed with `Module not found "file:///tmp/.../source/_shared/jwt.ts"`. Fix: Inspected the existing deployed `auth-signup-creator` via `get_edge_function` to see its 4-file structure (`source/index.ts`, `_shared/jwt.ts`, `_shared/cors.ts`, `_shared/apify.ts`). Built `/tmp/metrics-refresh-deploy.json` with all 4 files and intend to redeploy with the full payload.
   - **Unused import**: Initial `metrics-refresh/index.ts` imported `type SupabaseClient` and exported a dead `type _SupabaseClient = SupabaseClient;` workaround. Fixed: removed both via Edit.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Spec vs. story drift**: Story AC said throttle via `metric_snapshots.created_at` and body shape `{user_id, platform}`. Spec §5.2 says throttle via `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at` and body `{social_link_id}`. Per Ralph rule "specs win", followed spec and documented in progress.txt.
   - **snapshot_ids pre-row gap**: Spec expects pre-inserted `status='refreshing'` metric_snapshots rows with IDs returned. US-016/017/018 webhook RPCs only INSERT + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (no UPDATE-first path), so pre-rows would be orphaned. Decision: return `snapshot_ids: []`; UI relies on realtime subscription. Documented as spec gap consistent with US-020's handling.
   - **Throttle burn on dispatch failure**: Decision to bump `last_scrape_attempt_at` BEFORE Apify dispatch protects budget from spam via flaky paths at the cost of making the user wait 6h even if Apify was down during their first try — documented in the inline comment.
   - **Budget for smoke test**: Real Apify dispatch costs ~$0.16 for a full refresh. Plan is a cheap smoke test that covers structural branches (401/403/400/404/429) without burning Apify, plus a deferred `-live.ts` for end-to-end (matches US-016 convention).

6. All user messages:
   - Single initial invocation of the Ralph autonomous loop. The user message is effectively the Ralph instructions loaded as SessionStart hook context — no interactive follow-up messages from the user.
   - System reminders from tool infrastructure: (a) using-superpowers skill content (invoke skills before responding), (b) deferred tools available via ToolSearch, (c) MCP server instructions for context7 / supabase / mobile, (d) available skills list, (e) project CLAUDE.md + scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md with the 11-step loop, (f) periodic TodoWrite reminders, (g) notice that LSP tool became unavailable, (h) final "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY" directive for summary generation.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Redeploy `metrics-refresh` edge function via `mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__deploy_edge_function` with all 4 files (`source/index.ts` + `_shared/jwt.ts` + `_shared/cors.ts` + `_shared/apify.ts`) and `verify_jwt: false`
   - Write smoke test `scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts` covering: missing auth (401), malformed JWT (401), lister-role JWT (403), malformed JSON (400), non-UUID social_link_id (400), non-owned link (404), fresh call then immediate second call (200 → 429 with retry_after_sec)
   - Re-run quality gates (tsc, lint, supabase advisors)
   - Spawn `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with diff scope + US-021 AC + docs/tech-architecture.md §5.2 + §1.6 pointers
   - Commit with message `feat: [US-021] Edge function: metrics-refresh with 6h throttle`
   - Set `US-021.passes: true` in prd.json
   - Append dated entry to progress.txt (include learnings + spec-gap notes: snapshot_ids deferred, body-shape spec-wins, bump-before-dispatch tradeoff)
   - Update `.env.example` if any new env vars were added (none in this case — all reused)
   - Final response: not COMPLETE yet (US-022 through US-065 remain) — end normally

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before the summary request, I was in the middle of step 2 of the loop (deploy edge function). The `metrics-refresh/index.ts` file is written and passes tsc + lint. The deploy JSON payload `/tmp/metrics-refresh-deploy.json` was just built via a node one-liner that produced 22,773 bytes containing 4 files. The last output was: `wrote 22773 bytes`. The next action was going to be calling `mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__deploy_edge_function` with the full 4-file array. TodoWrite state: task "Deploy edge function via Supabase MCP" is `in_progress`.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Redeploy `metrics-refresh` with all four files bundled. The todo item literally reads "Deploy edge function via Supabase MCP" and is `in_progress`. The deploy call should use:
   - `project_id: "[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]"`
   - `name: "metrics-refresh"`
   - `entrypoint_path: "source/index.ts"`
   - `verify_jwt: false`
   - `files: [<contents of /tmp/metrics-refresh-deploy.json>]` — 4 entries with names `source/index.ts`, `_shared/jwt.ts`, `_shared/cors.ts`, `_shared/apify.ts`
   
   From the conversation: *"Let me build the deploy payload with all four files using a bash helper."* followed by the successful `wrote 22773 bytes` output. The payload is ready at `/tmp/metrics-refresh-deploy.json`; the next concrete action is to read it back (or inline it) and call deploy_edge_function. After deploy succeeds, proceed to write the smoke test, run it, then run the code-reviewer subagent, commit, and mark US-021 `passes: true`.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/bulutyazgan/.claude/projects/-Users-bulutyazgan-Desktop-marketify/551b4671-3a22-47be-a96d-ff63924ec1d0.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.
